basics.name: Marc Robertson
basics.label: Computer Scientist
basics.summary: Undergradute senior, residing and pursuing a degree in Computer Science at Oakland City University. Passionate about software development, machine learning, and physical computing. Eager to apply my skills and knowledge in real-world projects and contribute to innovative solutions.

work.name: Institute for Technology, Oakland City University
work.summary: Troubleshooting employee issues, while providing support for the department. In addition, keeping the systems updated and running smoothly.

volunteer.summary: Lead strength and conditioning coach for the Midwest Hooligans Football Club. Very committed to improving the athletic performance of all players through tailored training programs and motivational coaching.
highlights: Awarded 'FIT Champ' award by the Hooligans for quality Fit standards set for players.

awards.title: Presidents List
awards.summary: The President's List is relative to what GPA you acquired and is expected to have a GPA of 3.8 and above. For four semesters I have been awarded this accolade.

references.name: Professor Zack Wilson
references.reference: Marc Robertson is a dedicated and motivated student who consistently demonstrates strong problem-solving skills, technical ability, and a positive work ethic. He approaches tasks with professionalism and shows a clear willingness to learn and improve. I am confident he will be a valuable asset in any academic or professional environment.

references.name: Professor Adam Price
references.reference: Marc Robertson has shown excellent commitment to his studies and a strong understanding of technical concepts. He works well independently and in team settings, communicates clearly, and takes initiative when faced with challenges. I highly recommend him for future academic or professional opportunities.

projects.name: Charity Navigator
projects.summary: My project's goal is to link local charities with bigger and national charities. In efforts to help raise awareness and funds for local causes.
